game_name: Might and Magic III: Isles of Terra

element_introduction_order.element: Turn-based combat with visible enemy approach
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental system; players encounter enemies approaching from distance, allowing tactical positioning

element_introduction_order.element: Ranged weapons and tactical distance combat
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Distinct tactical advantage system replacing melee-only early gameplay
Improvement over numeric code entry; gradual spell acquisition through level progression

element_introduction_order.element: Multiple flat 'nacelle' worlds with distinct environments
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Travel between separate island worlds, each with unique challenges and lore

element_introduction_order.element: Elemental Lords and Forces of the Dome encounters
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive revelation through Corak's journals; escalating supernatural threats
Voluntary high-stakes challenge; cannot save game state, raises tension significantly
Mandatory progression point with permanent consequences
Final major obstacle before endgame; grants 'Ultimate Adventurers' title upon completion

element_introduction_order.element: Pyramids of the Ancients and Main Control Center
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Reveals world-building secrets; underwater seedship setting
End-game transportation; leads directly to Might and Magic IV

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching save points (inns in previous tradition)
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching visible enemy parties from distance
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation with safe-zone punctuation; tension builds through world-traversal, peaks at mandatory no-save dungeons and final sequences, releases through town/world transitions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Separate nacelle worlds allow optional exploration; players can choose traversal order between multiple island realms
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Corak's journals provide lore breadcrumbs to Elemental Lords and Forces of the Dome, encouraging thorough exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial intensity in Fountain Head starting town; basic turn-based combat familiarization
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating enemy encounters across multiple nacelle worlds; introduction of Elemental Lords and supernatural threats
difficulty_curve.late_game: Extreme spike: Arena (optional but high-stakes), Castle Greywind (mandatory no-save), Maze from Hell (permadeath threats), culminating in endgame revelations
notable_spikes: Maze from Hell (final major gauntlet before endgame)
Sheltem final confrontation and escape sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Fountain Head starting town serves as safe hub for character management, spell selection, and resource gathering before world traversal
Separate flat 'nacelle' worlds require traversal between island realms; each world contains distinct enemies, environments, and lore tied to Sheltem's machinations
Arena and Castle Greywind function as high-stakes progression gates where game state cannot be saved; forces commitment to tactical decisions
Maze from Hell → Pyramids of the Ancients → Main Control Center creates triple-layer endgame culmination; each layer reveals deeper world-building secrets
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Maze from Hell, Pyramids, underwater seedship
